issue: Division is meant to truncate toward zero, the way an integer calculator does, so that a division with a negative result drops its fractional part rather than rounding down. At the moment some negative quotients come out one too low: dividing when exactly one operand is negative rounds away from zero instead of toward it. Divisions that are exact, all-positive, or between two same-sign operands are fine. Make the division operator truncate toward zero.
